engineIntelligence.h2: Suzuki Kei Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Suzuki Kei in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for JDM grey imports.

cards.altText: Suzuki Kei K6A-T engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo bearing wear - premature failure from 60k miles causes oil smoke and boost loss on high-revving units.
Intercooler piping leak - silicone hose cracking leads to boost pressure drops and hesitation.
Timing chain tensioner wear - hydraulic tensioner fatigue causes startup rattle and valve timing drift.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,400-£2,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable K6A-T JDM units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Suzuki Kei K6A engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption - piston ring wear after 80k miles increases blow-by and requires frequent top-ups.
VVT solenoid failure - variable timing valve clogs causing rough idle and reduced fuel economy.
Camshaft lobe wear - high-mileage units show premature lobe rounding from aggressive oil degradation.

cards.altText: Suzuki Kei K6A-Z engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Immobiliser sync issues - replacement ECU requires JDM protocol pairing to prevent no-start faults.
Cooling system leaks - radiator seam and water pump failure common on pre-2006 HN21 chassis units.
Injector clogging - ultra-small bore injectors foul from inconsistent UK fuel quality and short trips.

engineGuide.h2: Suzuki Kei Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

entries.title: 0.7L Petrol
entries.history: The foundation of the Kei range, introduced in 1998 with aluminium construction and SOHC efficiency for lightweight urban driving.
compatibleVariants: X, G, 2WD Base, Early HN Daily
commonFailures: Piston ring wear - oil consumption exceeds 1L/1000 miles past 80k, requiring ring replacement or block rebuild.
Timing chain tensioner fatigue - cold-start rattle precedes chain stretch and potential valve timing misalignment.

entries.title: 0.7L Petrol Turbo

compatibleVariants: S, RS, Track Spec, Turbo Enthusiast Builds
commonFailures: Turbo bearing wear - delayed oil drain-back under high G-forces causes shaft play and compressor failure.

commonFailures: Immobiliser sync errors - replacement ECU requires JDM protocol pairing to prevent crank-no-start faults.
Cooling system degradation - radiator seam and water pump failures common on early HN21 units.

Injector clogging - ultra-small bore injectors foul rapidly on inconsistent UK fuel during short trips.

commonProblems.h2: Suzuki Kei Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Suzuki Kei’s high-revving 658cc K6A family delivers exceptional performance but places significant stress on internal components during enthusiastic UK driving. Turbocharger bearing failure, timing chain tensioner wear, and piston ring degradation dominate replacement enquiries for HN and HN21 chassis. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to make an informed repair versus replacement decision.

problems.h4: Suzuki Kei Turbocharger Failure - Why K6A-T Engines Lose Boost Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Kei S, RS Turbo, HN21 Turbo (1998-2009, K6A-T & K6A-ZT)
problems.rootCause: Oil degradation and delayed drain-back under high-G spirited driving starve the turbo bearings, leading to shaft play, compressor wheel contact, and eventual seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Core rebuild with new bearings, seals, and cartridge balancing.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if shaft play is minimal. Extends life 20k-30k miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Direct bolt-on exchange turbo, pre-oiled, includes intercooler hose check.
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable fix. Matches factory boost calibration perfectly.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete K6A-T swap with upgraded oil feed lines, intercooler, and ECU sync.
repairOptions.longevity: Best for catastrophic metal ingress into engine or manifold. Lifetime solution.

problems.recommendation: If the engine still turns over smoothly with no knocking, a direct turbo swap is the most cost-effective route. We advise fitting reinforced oil feed lines simultaneously to prevent repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K6A-T turbo engine prices for your Suzuki Kei

problems.group: Suzuki Kei Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Suzuki Kei Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why K6A Chains Stretch
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Kei X, G, S (All chassis 1998-2009, K6A & K6A-Z)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner fluid degradation and high-RPM valve float fatigue stretch the guide rails, causing cold-start rattle and eventual valve timing misalignment.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full chain, tensioner, guides, and VVT gear replacement.
repairOptions.longevity: Highly recommended at 60k miles. Prevents catastrophic engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, block inspection, and installation of upgraded K6A unit.
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if chain skip has already bent valves.

problems.recommendation: Addressing chain tension at 60,000 miles saves thousands. Once rattle is audible on startup, the chain has likely jumped; immediate specialist replacement is critical to avoid valve damage.
problems.cta: Find K6A timing chain replacement quotes for your Suzuki Kei

problems.h4: Suzuki Kei Oil Consumption Problem - Why K6A Pistons Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Kei Daily, Enthusiast, Track (1998-2009, All K6A variants)
problems.rootCause: High-revving operation breaks down thin factory piston rings, allowing oil to bypass into the combustion chamber, increasing carbon buildup and spark plug fouling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve cover gaskets, PCV valve, and oil separator replacement.
repairOptions.longevity: Stops external leaks but does not address internal ring wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full block rebuild or swap with heavy-duty HN-spec piston rings.
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive fix. Eliminates consumption for 100k+ miles.

problems.recommendation: If consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles, top-ups and catch cans are temporary. A rebuilt unit with upgraded piston rings restores compression and prevents costly catalytic converter damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Kei oil consumption engine rebuilds

problems.h4: Suzuki Kei Immobiliser Sync Failure - Why Grey Import ECUs Reject Keys
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Kei HN21, Late Model (2004-2009, K6A-Z & K6A-ZT)
problems.rootCause: JDM ECU replacement lacks UK DVLA-compliant immobiliser pairing. Mismatched transponder codes trigger fuel cut and crank-no-start conditions.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - ECU Sync & Key Programming
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: JDM diagnostic protocol pairing, transponder cloning, and immobiliser flash.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix. Required for all post-2004 Kei engine swaps.

problems.recommendation: Never install a used HN21 engine without pre-verified ECU sync. Specialist pairing ensures DVLA registration compliance and prevents mid-drive security lockouts.
problems.cta: Secure Suzuki Kei ECU immobiliser sync service quotes

problems.h4: Suzuki Kei Intercooler Piping Leak - Why K6A-T Boost Drops
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Kei S, RS, Track (1998-2009, K6A-T)
problems.rootCause: Factory rubber couplers crack under sustained boost pressure and heat cycling, causing hissing leaks and immediate lean-running conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Direct-fit reinforced silicone piping with T-bolt clamps.
repairOptions.longevity: Solves 90% of boost leaks. Lasts 40k+ miles under track use.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete intercooler core, piping, and turbo replacement.
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if unboosted running has already overheated valves.

problems.recommendation: Silicone hose upgrades are mandatory for spirited K6A-T use. Inspect clamps every 12 months to prevent sudden boost loss during high-speed overtaking.
problems.cta: Compare K6A-T intercooler piping repair costs UK

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Suzuki Kei
finalCta.paragraph: The Suzuki Kei’s high-performance JDM engineering demands specialist attention when failures occur. EngineMarket connects you directly with accredited grey import garages and precision rebuilders who understand K6A timing, DVLA compliance, and turbo calibration. Every replacement unit includes upgraded failure-point components, bench testing, and nationwide delivery. Compare prices now to secure a verified specialist and avoid main dealer markups on ultra-niche components.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Suzuki Kei Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Suzuki Kei and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Suzuki Kei Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Suzuki Kei is listed below, with compatible engine codes, variant years and common failure notes to ensure precise replacement matching.

items.title: Suzuki Kei 0.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Suzuki Kei was exclusively powered by the 658cc three-cylinder K6A family, engineered to maximise performance within Japan’s strict kei car displacement regulations. Fitted across all HN and HN21 chassis from 1998 to 2009, this lightweight aluminium block delivered exceptional power-to-weight ratios and formed the backbone of the model’s enthusiast appeal. Available in naturally aspirated and intercooled turbo configurations, it remains highly sought after for grey import compliance and track-focused builds.
commonFailurePoints: piston ring oil bypass on high-revving units.

engineYears.h2: Suzuki Kei Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Suzuki Kei’s production lifecycle, organised so UK owners can quickly identify their K6A variant and compare rebuilt prices from accredited specialists.

years.description: The Kei debuted with the original K6A family, establishing a lightweight 658cc platform that defined UK grey import performance.
keyChanges: Introduction of SOHC K6A naturally aspirated engine for base trims
K6A-T intercooled turbo launch for S and RS performance variants
knownFor: Lightweight aluminium construction delivering exceptional power-to-weight ratios
Early turbo reliability praised for track potential but vulnerable to oil neglect
High enthusiast demand driving rapid grey import adoption in the UK
Timing chain guides wearing prematurely under sustained high-RPM operation
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998 Suzuki Kei replacement engines
years.ticker: Suzuki Kei K6A-T turbo failure • Suzuki Kei 1998 engine replacement • K6A timing chain tensioner fix

years.description: The HN21 chassis arrived with revised K6A-Z and K6A-ZT engines, featuring improved emissions compliance and updated ECU mapping.
keyChanges: Launch of K6A-Z NA engine with revised cooling passages
K6A-ZT turbo integration with updated boost solenoids
Chassis stiffening and interior redesign for late-model compliance
knownFor: Improved thermal management reducing early overheating reports
ECU immobiliser integration complicating non-specialist engine swaps
Strong collector interest in low-mileage late-model grey imports
VVT solenoid calibration sensitivity requiring precise oil maintenance
years.cta: Compare HN21 chassis Suzuki Kei engine prices

Discontinuation of early K6A-T in favour of ZT specifications
knownFor: Peak enthusiast demand driving premium aftermarket and rebuild support
High-revving valve train durability improved through late TSB revisions
Strong ULEZ and clean air zone exemption status due to age
Rare low-mileage examples commanding collector market premiums

faq.h2: Suzuki Kei Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Suzuki Kei engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Prices typically range from £1,100 to £2,400 for supply-only rebuilt K6A units, depending on turbo specification and chassis generation. Fully fitted packages with specialist labour and DVLA compliance add £450-£850.
keyPoints: Naturally aspirated K6A rebuilds start around £1,100-£1,800.
K6A-T turbo replacements average £1,400-£2,200 due to upgraded internals.
Late-model K6A-Z/ZT units cost slightly more for ECU calibration.
Supply and fit adds £450-£850 depending on chassis complexity.
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Kei engine replacement prices today

items.question: What engine codes fit the Suzuki Kei?
items.answer: The Kei exclusively uses the K6A family: K6A (NA), K6A-T (Turbo), K6A-Z (Late NA), and K6A-ZT (Late Turbo). Exact compatibility depends on HN or HN21 chassis year and ECU generation.
keyPoints: K6A and K6A-T power 1998-2003 first-generation HN models.
K6A-Z and K6A-ZT are fitted to 2004-2009 HN21 chassis.
Cross-generation swaps require full ECU and immobiliser reprogramming.
Turbo and NA variants are not directly interchangeable without wiring harness changes.
items.cta: Check your Suzuki Kei engine code compatibility now

items.question: How long does a Suzuki Kei engine replacement take?
items.answer: Specialist supply and fit typically takes 5-7 working days, including JDM engine sourcing, ECU immobiliser pairing, and DVLA compliance documentation.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing from verified Japanese stock takes 2-3 days.
Physical installation and harness integration requires 1-2 days.
ECU sync and road testing add 1 day for complete sign-off.
Complex 4WD turbo conversions may extend timeline to 8 days.
items.cta: Get a Suzuki Kei engine installation time quote

items.question: Do Suzuki Kei replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all engines supplied through our network include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering internal mechanical failures and manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: Rebuilt and reconditioned units include comprehensive parts coverage.
Warranty is valid for both daily driving and enthusiast track use.
Claims require proof of specialist installation and correct oil grades.
Turbo bearing failures are covered if oil supply lines meet spec.
items.cta: View Suzuki Kei engine warranty terms here

items.question: Can I fit a used Suzuki Kei engine myself?
items.answer: While physically possible for experienced mechanics, DIY fitting is strongly discouraged due to mandatory ECU immobiliser pairing and DVLA registration compliance requirements.
keyPoints: JDM ECUs require proprietary diagnostic protocol pairing.
Incorrect immobiliser sync causes immediate crank-no-start faults.
DVLA documentation must be verified by an accredited specialist.
DIY installs void manufacturer warranty and complicate insurance claims.
items.cta: Find an accredited Suzuki Kei engine fitter near you

items.question: What causes Suzuki Kei engine failure?
items.answer: High-revving enthusiast use accelerates timing chain tensioner wear, turbo bearing degradation, and piston ring oil bypass, particularly when UK maintenance intervals are extended.
keyPoints: Delayed oil changes starve turbo bearings and degrade chain tensioners.
Factory rubber intercooler couplers crack under sustained boost pressure.
High mileage breaks down thin piston rings, increasing combustion blow-by.
Ignoring cold-start chain rattle leads to catastrophic valve timing skip.
items.cta: Diagnose your Suzuki Kei engine failure symptoms

items.question: Is a reconditioned Suzuki Kei engine reliable?
items.answer: Reconditioned K6A units are highly reliable when rebuilt by JDM specialists who upgrade known weak points like chain tensioners, piston rings, and turbo oil feed lines.
keyPoints: Full teardown eliminates hidden fatigue from previous high-rev use.
Upgraded components exceed original factory durability specifications.
Bench testing guarantees compression and oil pressure before dispatch.
Ideal for collectors demanding show-car reliability and performance.
items.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Kei reconditioned engines

items.question: How do I know which Suzuki Kei engine I need?
items.answer: Cross-reference your chassis code (HN or HN21), production year, and existing ECU label to identify the exact K6A variant required for seamless installation.
keyPoints: Early 1998-2003 models require K6A or K6A-T engines.
Late 2004-2009 models require K6A-Z or K6A-ZT specifications.
Turbo and NA harnesses are incompatible without specialist conversion.
Use our free registration checker for instant chassis verification.
items.cta: Identify your Suzuki Kei engine instantly

items.question: Can I upgrade my Suzuki Kei engine to a turbo version?
items.answer: Yes, NA to turbo conversions are possible but require a complete K6A-T swap, upgraded fuel system, intercooler plumbing, and custom ECU mapping for reliable operation.
keyPoints: K6A-T swap requires reinforced engine mounts and exhaust manifolds.
Fuel injectors and pump must be upgraded for higher demand.
Intercooler piping and boost solenoids need direct-fit replacement.
Professional dyno tuning is mandatory for safe boost calibration.
items.cta: Explore Suzuki Kei turbo conversion specialist quotes

items.question: What’s included in a Suzuki Kei engine supply and fit package?
items.answer: Packages include a verified rebuilt K6A unit, specialist installation, ECU immobiliser pairing, full fluid calibration, and complete DVLA registration support documentation.
keyPoints: Pre-tested engine with upgraded failure-point components.
Professional harness integration and coolant/oil flush.
JDM diagnostic pairing ensures no-start faults are prevented.
Road testing and compliance certificates provided upon completion.
